title: melica ciliata subsp. monticola
intro: Melica ciliata subsp. monticola, also known as mountain melick, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, where it grows in rocky, mountainous terrain. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The roots of the plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, offering potential health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and may be used to support the immune system and aid in the treatment of various infections.
